qualifying_org_definition: Any public or private nonprofit entity where the ABAWD performs volunteer/unpaid work. The Mississippi SNAP Policy Manual (Vol. V, Ch. 3, pp. 3277–3279) explicitly treats 'volunteer or unpaid work' at nonprofits as a qualifying ABAWD work activity (the 'non-referable' lane at 80 hrs/month). No state-imposed pre-approved site list, MOU, or provider registration is required for the general volunteer lane. (A separate 'comparable workfare' track for ABAWDs below 80 hrs/month does require county director site approval and an FLSA-calculated hour assignment, but that is a distinct workfare track, not the general volunteer lane Tended uses.)
verification_instrument: Caseworker verification of hours reported by the volunteer organization; caseworker codes 'WO' (Work Requirements Met) in the MAVERICS ABBA screen and documents in the Case Documentation (CADO) screen. No specific Mississippi-named state form for volunteer/community service hour verification was identified; the volunteer provides organization-attested hours to the caseworker. MDHS EA Form 910 is for employment wage verification only and does not apply to volunteer hours.
hours_formula_detail: Mississippi SNAP Policy Manual explicitly states 'volunteer or unpaid work (80 hours monthly or an average of 20 hours weekly)' to qualify as a non-referable ABAWD. Follows federal 7 CFR 273.24 baseline exactly.
accepts_outofstate_detail: The Mississippi SNAP Policy Manual and MDHS guidance do not address the geographic location of the certifying nonprofit. State policy defines qualifying orgs as 'any public or private nonprofit entity' with no in-state restriction stated. Mississippi does require charitable solicitation registration with the Secretary of State for orgs soliciting in-state, but that is a separate fundraising regulation and has no bearing on SNAP ABAWD verification. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) accepts any qualifying nonprofit regardless of location; absent a state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Mississippi did not hold any active ABAWD time-limit waivers as of FY2024 Q4 and has none in 2026. OBBBA eliminated the area-unemployment-rate waiver authority; no Mississippi counties are waived.
submission_deadline: unknown — federal rules prohibit states from requiring ABAWDs to report hours every month under simplified reporting; recipients must report if hours drop below 20/week. Practically, hours are presented to the caseworker at recertification or at the time of an interview. No Mississippi-specific monthly deadline documented.
notes:
Mississippi administers SNAP at the state level through MDHS with no county-administered structure; no statewide ABAWD waiver is in effect (Q4 2024 confirmed; OBBBA 2025 expands coverage). Evidence indicates a clean general volunteer lane: volunteer work at nonprofits counts toward 80 hrs/month, verified by a signed timesheet from the organization's authorized representative, with no mandatory county assignment, EDP, provider registration, or MOU required. Confidence is medium because the Mississippi SNAP Policy Manual PDF (sos.ms.gov) and the MDHS manual (RevisedSNAPManual-10.20.pdf) were not machine-readable in this session; no explicit named state verification form for volunteer hours was found, and the MDHS client-facing forms page lists only an employment wage form (EA Form 910).

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial review conducted. The Mississippi SNAP Policy Manual (RevisedSNAPManual-10.20.pdf, Vol. V, Chapter 3, pp. 3277-3352; extracted via pdfminer from a locally saved copy) was successfully machine-read and the ABAWD policy sections were directly reviewed. Key findings that confirm and upgrade the Tier 1 classification: (1) The manual explicitly defines 'Non-Referable ABAWDs' as including 'Individuals meeting the ABAWD work requirement through employment, in-kind income, volunteer or unpaid work (80 hours monthly or an average of 20 hours weekly).' The policy states: 'Do not refer an individual to E&T if that individual is meeting the ABAWD work requirement through work as defined in the above statement.' (2) The illustrative example (p. 3277): 'Joe Smith's church runs a daily feeding program and he volunteers for 25 hours per week in the kitchen. Work hours are verified by the worker. He is meeting the ABAWD work requirement and is not a referable ABAWD.' This is the operative Tended use case — a nonprofit volunteer ≥20 hrs/week is non-referable with no EDP, no E&T enrollment, and no prestep required. (3) The county director site approval requirement is specific to 'comparable workfare' — a lower-hourly-burden FLSA-calculated track for ABAWDs who cannot meet 80 hrs/month on their own and are referred to E&T. It does not apply to ABAWDs already meeting 80 hrs/month through volunteer work. (4) Regular workfare requires a signed MDHS-EA-566 Workfare Agreement between the county director and the sponsor; again, this is only the E&T workfare track, not the general volunteer lane. (5) Searches for mandatory EDP enrollment, pre-approved nonprofit lists, MOU requirements, and Skills2Work mandatory enrollment for the general volunteer track found no such requirements. Skills2Work/E&T is only triggered when an ABAWD is 'referable' (i.e., not meeting 80 hrs/month through work/volunteering). (6) The Mississippi Today article (Dec 2025) was paywalled/403 but other sources confirmed no state-specific restrictions beyond federal 7 CFR 273.24. Confidence upgraded to 'high' based on direct policy manual text resolving all material gaps: the general volunteer lane is confirmed clean (no EDP, no E&T referral, no site pre-approval, no MOU), and hours_reporting remains self_reported in the sense that the ABAWD provides organization-attested hours to their caseworker who codes WO in MAVERICS. No new adverse findings from adversarial search.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: MS policy manual text explicitly uses '80 hours monthly or an average of 20 hours weekly' — confirmed federal_80 baseline, monthly_hours_required=80. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: MS policy is silent on the geographic location of certifying orgs; state policy says 'any public or private nonprofit entity' with no in-state restriction; MS charitable solicitation registration rules are separate from SNAP verification and do not restrict ABAWD qualifying orgs; accepts_outofstate_nonprofit='silent'. (3) Geographic waivers: MS has no active ABAWD waivers in 2026; OBBBA eliminated waiver authority for area-unemployment-rate waivers; statewide enforcement confirmed. (4) Submission UX: No state-specific online portal for ABAWD hour reporting found; recipients present organization-attested hours to caseworker at county office (paper_county_office); federal simplified-reporting rules prohibit mandatory monthly reporting — hours changes (drops below 20/week) must be reported but routine reporting is at recertification; no named monthly deadline. (5) Form fields: No named MS state form for volunteer hour verification; applying the default reasonable baseline of org_name, org_signature, supervisor_name, recipient_name, hours_total, activity_description, period_start_date, period_end_date. The EA Form 910 covers employment wages only and does not apply.
